scenarios.title: Flat rate margin calculated with higher of long or short positions included per sub-margin group
scenarios.intent: Verify that flat rate margin is correctly calculated by including only positions from the side with higher absolute market value for each sub-margin group and applying the margin multiplier
given: A clearing participant has positions across multiple sub-margin groups defined on HKEX website
Sub-group 1: Long positions total absolute market value 30,000,000 HKD, Short positions total absolute market value 60,000,000 HKD
Sub-group 2: Long positions total absolute market value 750,000 HKD, Short positions total absolute market value 0 HKD
Sub-group 3: Long positions total absolute market value 0 HKD, Short positions total absolute market value 300,000 HKD
Flat margin rates under FieldType 3 are 12% for sub-group 1, 30% for sub-group 2, 55% for sub-group 3
A flat rate margin multiplier of 2 is assigned per DWH0081C report
when: The flat rate margin calculation is performed following Step 1 (compare long vs short absolute market values per sub-margin group), Step 2 (sum product of included positions' absolute market value and flat margin rate), and Step 3 (apply margin multiplier)
then: Sub-group 1: Short positions included (60,000,000 > 30,000,000), long positions excluded
Sub-group 2: Long positions included (750,000 > 0), short positions excluded
Sub-group 3: Short positions included (300,000 > 0), long positions excluded
Flat rate margin before multiplier = 60,000,000 × 12% + 750,000 × 30% + 300,000 × 55% = 7,590,000 HKD
Flat rate margin after applying margin multiplier = 7,590,000 × 2 = 15,180,000 HKD

evidence.quote: take the higher of long and short absolute market value for each of them
all short sub-group 1 positions will be included in the flat rate margin calculation while all long sub-group 1 positions will be excluded
Flat rate margin after applying margin multiplier = (60,000,000 × 12% + 750,000 × 30% + 300,000 × 55%) × 2 = 15,180,000

scenarios.title: Flat rate margin when sub-margin group has positions only on one side
scenarios.intent: Verify that flat rate margin correctly handles sub-margin groups where positions exist only on one side (all long or all short), with zero value on the opposite side
given: A clearing participant has positions in a sub-margin group
The sub-margin group has only long positions with total absolute market value of 750,000 HKD
The sub-margin group has zero short positions (total absolute market value of 0 HKD)
Flat margin rate under FieldType 3 is 30% for this sub-margin group
when: The flat rate margin calculation compares long and short absolute market values for the sub-margin group
then: Long positions are included in flat rate margin calculation (750,000 > 0)
Short positions are excluded since their total absolute market value is zero
Flat rate margin contribution from this sub-margin group = 750,000 × 30% = 225,000 HKD

evidence.quote: all long sub-group 2 positions... will be included in the flat rate margin calculation
take the higher of long and short absolute market value for each of them

scenarios.title: Flat rate margin calculation validates required input data
scenarios.intent: Verify that all required data elements are present and valid for flat rate margin calculation including positions, sub-margin groups, flat margin rates, and margin multiplier
given: A clearing participant submits positions for flat rate margin calculation
when: The system validates input data including positions identified per §3.2.2, sub-margin groups from HKEX website, flat margin rates under FieldType 3, and margin multiplier from DWH0081C report
then: All required data elements are validated as present and correctly formatted
Position identification per §3.2.2 is confirmed
Sub-margin group assignments are validated against HKEX website definitions
Flat margin rates under FieldType 3 are retrieved successfully
Margin multiplier from DWH0081C report is retrieved successfully

evidence.quote: each position identified as per instructions in §3.2.2
each sub-margin group of flat rate margin calculations defined on HKEX website
flat margin rate under FieldType 3
flat rate margin multiplier by referring to the Daily Participant Margin Multiplier Report (DWH0081C)

scenarios.title: Aggregated margin rounded up to nearest rounding parameter
scenarios.intent: Verify that aggregated market-risk-component margin is correctly calculated by summing all components and rounding up to the nearest rounding parameter
given: Portfolio margin is 10,000,000 HKD
Flat rate margin is 15,180,000 HKD
Liquidation risk add-on is 266,865 HKD
Structured product add-on is 550,000 HKD
Corporate action position margin is 2,500,000 HKD
Holiday add-on is 18,433,039 HKD
Rounding parameter in Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000
when: The aggregated market-risk-component margin is calculated and rounded up
then: Aggregated margin before rounding equals 46,929,904 HKD
Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin equals 46,930,000 HKD

evidence.quote: Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on = 46,929,904
46,929,904 is to be rounded up to the nearest 10,000. Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin = 46,930,000

scenarios.title: Rounding when aggregated margin is exact multiple of rounding parameter
scenarios.intent: Verify rounding behavior when aggregated margin is already an exact multiple of the rounding parameter
given: Aggregated market-risk-component margin before rounding is exactly 50,000,000 HKD
Rounding parameter in Initial Margin Risk Parameter File is 10,000
when: The rounding up to nearest 10,000 is applied
then: Rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin remains 50,000,000 HKD
No upward adjustment is needed as value is already at rounding boundary
assumptions: When value is already at exact multiple, rounding up should not increase the value

evidence.quote: Round up the aggregated margin with reference to the rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File (e.g., 10,000)

scenarios.title: Aggregated margin calculation validates all component data
scenarios.intent: Verify that all required margin components are present and the rounding parameter is correctly retrieved from the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File
given: A clearing participant has margin components to be aggregated
when: The system validates presence of Portfolio margin, Flat rate margin, Liquidation risk add-on, Structured product add-on, Corporate action position margin, and Holiday add-on
The system retrieves the rounding parameter from the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File
then: All six margin components are validated as present with numeric values
Rounding parameter is retrieved successfully from Initial Margin Risk Parameter File
Rounding parameter is a positive integer value

evidence.quote: Aggregated market-risk-component margin = Portfolio margin + Flat rate margin + Liquidation risk add-on + Structured product add-on + Corporate action position margin + Holiday add-on
rounding parameter stated in the Initial Margin Risk Parameter File

scenarios.title: Calculate favorable MTM using portfolio values
scenarios.intent: Verify the favorable MTM calculation produces correct results when market value differs from contract value
given: A clearing participant has a portfolio with Market valuePortfolio of -287,850,000 HKD
The portfolio has Contract valuePortfolio of -300,550,000 HKD
when: The system calculates favorable MTM using the formula: Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
then: The favorable MTM is calculated as (-287,850,000) - (-300,550,000) = 12,700,000
A positive result indicates favorable MTM
assumptions: Portfolio values are provided in HKD equivalent
Rounding is applied at position level as per Appendix 4.6

evidence.quote: Favorable MTM (or MTM requirement) = Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio

scenarios.title: Boundary between favorable MTM and MTM requirement
scenarios.intent: Verify the system correctly distinguishes between favorable MTM and MTM requirement at the zero boundary
given: A clearing participant has a portfolio where Market valuePortfolio equals Contract valuePortfolio
when: The system calculates favorable MTM using the formula: Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
then: The favorable MTM is zero
The MTM requirement is zero
Net margin equals the rounded aggregated market-risk-component margin
assumptions: Zero result represents the boundary between favorable MTM and MTM requirement

evidence.quote: The negative number refers to a MTM requirement, which its absolute value will be added after applying margin credit

scenarios.title: Validate MTM requirement classification for negative result
scenarios.intent: Verify the system correctly classifies a negative calculation result as MTM requirement with favorable MTM set to zero
given: A clearing participant has a portfolio with Market valuePortfolio of -300,550,000 HKD
The portfolio has Contract valuePortfolio of -287,850,000 HKD
when: The system calculates favorable MTM using the formula: Market valuePortfolio - Contract valuePortfolio
then: The negative result is classified as MTM requirement
Favorable MTM is set to zero
The absolute value of MTM requirement (12,700,000) will be added after applying margin credit
assumptions: The absolute value is used for MTM requirement in the MTM and Margin Requirement Report

evidence.quote: The negative number refers to a MTM requirement, which its absolute value will be added after applying margin credit
In such case, favorable MTM is zero

scenarios.title: Apply margin credit to calculate net margin after credit
scenarios.intent: Verify the margin credit is correctly applied to net margin calculation
given: A clearing participant has a net margin of 46,930,000 HKD
The margin credit granted is 5,000,000 HKD
when: The system calculates net margin after credit using the formula: Maximum[0, (Net margin - Margin credit)]
then: Net margin after credit is calculated as Maximum[0, (46,930,000 - 5,000,000)] = 41,930,000
assumptions: Margin credit is normally 5,000,000 as specified
Net margin value is derived from prior calculation steps

evidence.quote: Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]

scenarios.title: Boundary case when net margin equals margin credit
scenarios.intent: Verify the Maximum function correctly handles the boundary when net margin equals margin credit
given: A clearing participant has a net margin of 5,000,000 HKD
The margin credit granted is 5,000,000 HKD
when: The system calculates net margin after credit using the formula: Maximum[0, (Net margin - Margin credit)]
then: Net margin after credit is calculated as Maximum[0, 0] = 0
assumptions: This tests the boundary condition where net margin exactly equals margin credit

evidence.quote: Net margin after credit = Maximum [0 , (Net margin – Margin credit)]

scenarios.title: Validate margin credit is granted to each clearing participant
scenarios.intent: Verify the margin credit value is properly assigned and applied for each clearing participant
given: A clearing participant is registered in the system
The standard margin credit value is 5,000,000 HKD
when: The system applies margin credit for margin calculation
then: The margin credit of 5,000,000 is granted to the clearing participant
The margin credit is applied in the net margin after credit calculation
assumptions: Margin credit value is normally 5,000,000 unless otherwise specified

evidence.quote: A margin credit (normally 5,000,000) is granted to each CP and applied for margin calculation

feature: Total MTM and Margin Requirement Calculation

scenarios.title: Calculate total MTM and margin requirement with all components
scenarios.intent: Verify that total MTM and margin requirement is correctly derived by summing net margin after credit and all other risk components
given: Net margin after credit is calculated as 41,930,000
MTM requirement is 12,700,000
Position limit add-on is 487,332
Credit risk add-on is 12,000,000
Ad-hoc add-on is 600,000
when: The system derives total MTM and margin requirement by adding net margin after credit to other risk components
then: Total MTM and margin requirement equals 67,717,332
assumptions: All component values are provided from prior calculation steps under §3.2.5 & §3.2.6

evidence.quote: Total MTM and margin requirement = Net margin after credit + Other risk components
= Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on

scenarios.title: Calculate total MTM and margin requirement when credit risk add-on is not applicable
scenarios.intent: Verify calculation handles the boundary condition where credit risk add-on is excluded upon VaR Platform launch
given: Net margin after credit is calculated as 41,930,000
MTM requirement is 12,700,000
Position limit add-on is 487,332
Credit risk add-on is not applicable due to VaR Platform launch
Ad-hoc add-on is 600,000
when: The system derives total MTM and margin requirement with credit risk add-on set to zero
then: Total MTM and margin requirement equals 55,717,332 (excluding credit risk add-on)
assumptions: VaR Platform has been launched and HKSCC has notified clearing participants
Credit risk add-on value is treated as zero when not applicable

evidence.quote: Please note that the credit risk add-on will not be applicable upon the launch of VaR Platform
Total MTM and margin requirement = Net margin after credit + Other risk components

scenarios.title: Validate all required risk components are present for total MTM calculation
scenarios.intent: Verify that the calculation validates presence and format of all required input components before deriving total MTM and margin requirement
given: A clearing participant has positions requiring margin calculation
Results from §3.2.5 and §3.2.6 calculations are available
when: The system validates that all required components are present: net margin after credit, MTM requirement, position limit add-on, credit risk add-on, and ad-hoc add-on
then: Validation confirms all components are present with valid numeric format
Calculation proceeds to derive total MTM and margin requirement
assumptions: Each component value must be a valid non-negative number
Missing or invalid components should prevent calculation

evidence.quote: total MTM and margin requirement could be derived by adding the net margin after credit to other risk components
= Net margin after credit + MTM requirement + Position limit add-on + Credit risk add-on + Ad-hoc add-on
